title: Variables: let, const
description: Use let and const correctly and understand reassignment rules.

concept.definition: TypeScript uses let for mutable bindings and const for non-reassignable bindings.
concept.analogy: const is a locked label; let is a writable label.
keyPoints: Use let for values that change

title: Core Types
description: Understand string, number, boolean, null, undefined, and unions.

concept.definition: TypeScript adds static types over JavaScript values.
concept.analogy: Types are contracts that catch misuse before runtime.

title: Functions
description: Type function parameters/returns and use optional/default params.

concept.definition: Function signatures document inputs and outputs and enable compile-time checks.
concept.analogy: A function type is a strict API form.

title: Control Flow
description: Branch with if/switch and leverage narrowing in conditions.

concept.definition: TypeScript narrows union types based on runtime checks.
concept.analogy: Checks act like filters that shrink possible types.

title: Loops
description: Use for, for...of, and array iteration methods effectively.

concept.definition: TypeScript supports standard JS loops with typed collections.
concept.analogy: Loops are controlled paths through a list of values.

title: Arrays & Tuples
description: Type arrays and fixed-shape tuples for safer indexed data.

concept.definition: Arrays hold variable-length same-type items; tuples hold fixed positions and types.
concept.analogy: Arrays are baskets; tuples are labeled slots.

title: Object Types
description: Define object shapes inline and use optional/readonly fields.

concept.definition: Object type annotations constrain required fields and value types.
concept.analogy: Object types are blueprints for JSON-like data.

defaultCode:
const user: { name: string; age?: number } = { name: "Mira" };
console.log(user.name);

title: Interfaces
description: Use interfaces for reusable object and function contracts.

concept.definition: Interfaces describe structural contracts that values must satisfy.
concept.analogy: An interface is a checklist for compatible shapes.

title: Classes
description: Create typed classes with access modifiers and methods.

concept.definition: TypeScript classes add typed properties, constructors, and visibility modifiers.
concept.analogy: Classes are templates for object instances with behavior.

title: Generics
description: Write reusable, type-safe functions and containers with type parameters.

concept.definition: Generics preserve type information across reusable abstractions.
concept.analogy: Generics are templates that lock in concrete types per use.

title: Async/Await
description: Handle asynchronous flows with Promise types and await.

concept.definition: async functions return Promise values; await unwraps fulfilled results.
concept.analogy: await pauses one function path until async work finishes.

title: Error Handling
description: Use try/catch safely and narrow unknown errors before reading properties.

concept.definition: TypeScript encourages safe error handling by treating caught errors as unknown in strict mode.
concept.analogy: Catch gives you an unlabeled box; narrow it before using contents.
keyPoints: Use try/catch for sync and async
Return typed failure states when useful
